role_title: Supply Chain Operations Program Manager, AI Infrastructure

location.office_time_required: Support onsite factory builds and field deployments as needed (domestic and international travel)

secondary_archetype: Supply Chain / NPI Operations Program Manager

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in Operations, Engineering, Program Management, or equivalent
Single-threaded operational leader accountable for outcomes end-to-end
Influence senior stakeholders and drive decisions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in Operations, Engineering, Program Management, or equivalent, within hardware development, manufacturing, or supply chain domains (compute, networking, datacenter, or similarly complex systems)
Proven track record leading complex hardware NPI programs end-to-end, from early bring-up through production ramp
Strong understanding of manufacturing and supply chain fundamentals, including BOM management, ECO/MCO processes, build readiness, factory test, quality controls, and material planning
Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams, influence senior stakeholders, and drive decisions in ambiguous, time-compressed environments
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to distill complex issues for executive and external audiences

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience operating in hyperscale datacenter or large-scale infrastructure environments
Familiarity with supplier engagement models, build cost drivers, and NPI scaling tradeoffs
Technical background in server, rack, and/or network hardware
Experience designing or improving operational processes, dashboards, governance forums, or execution metrics
Experience supporting PLM processes and familiarity with ERP systems

scope.surface: AI infrastructure hardware NPI and production ramps, including AI-native silicon and tightly integrated systems (e.g., Jalapeño and future generations)
primary_metrics_inferred: Production ramp throughput and yield targets
Scalability and repeatability of operating mechanisms
core_responsibilities: Act as single-threaded owner for operational readiness across NPI and ramp from early bring-up through sustained production
Translate infrastructure strategy and engineering objectives into operating plans, execution priorities, and decision frameworks
Drive alignment across Engineering, Operations, Strategic Sourcing, Finance, Capacity Planning, and Executive stakeholders
Lead transition activities from NPI to mass production
Translate engineering requirements into actionable, factory-ready plans with tier-1 manufacturing and integration partners
Lead cross-functional build and debug cadences
Drive factory and deployment ramp readiness including throughput, yield, quality controls, and reliability gates
Own end-to-end risk management—identification, mitigation planning, escalation, and executive-level communication
Build and scale repeatable operating mechanisms for predictable hardware development
Define requirements for operational systems (e.g., PLM enablement) and implement them
Build relationships with contract manufacturers, OEMs, and integrators
Support onsite factory builds and field deployments (domestic and international travel)

green_flags: Clear and specific role scope — single-threaded owner for NPI and production ramp
Named hardware program context (Jalapeño) signals real hardware work, not vague AI hype
Explicit travel expectation disclosed (domestic and international)
Detailed must-have and nice-to-have breakdown signals mature hiring process
OpenAI's infrastructure scale offers significant impact opportunity
red_flags: No city or location explicitly stated in the JD body — work location/hybrid policy unclear
Role requires international travel with no clarity on frequency or expectations
No mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and executive accountability signals)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no people management mentioned)
location.type (inferred as onsite from travel and factory build support language; no explicit office policy stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false given onsite factory and field deployment expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred as internal infrastructure from context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
ambiguities: Location/city not specified in JD body; no hybrid/remote policy stated. Treated as onsite given repeated references to onsite factory builds and field deployments.
Visa sponsorship policy not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as unclear.

The 'You Might Thrive in this role if' heading is used for required qualifications — language suggests conditional but items are structurally required. All items under this section treated as hard must-haves per JD language convention.
